title: Campylospermum congestum
taxonomy: Campylospermum congestum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It belongs to the genus Campylospermum, which is a group of plants native to tropical Africa. The species is classified as a part of the family Ochnaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Chaff Tree family. Campylospermum congestum is further classified as a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The classification of Campylospermum congestum is also reflected in its botanical name, which includes its genus, species, and family.

intro: Campylospermum congestum is a plant species that belongs to the family Ochnaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Campylospermum congestum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
